body_txt: Astonishing as it may seem, not all non- Muggle characters — be those wizards, aliens, robots, or anything else — are indifferent to or on a crusade to murder us ordinary humans. In fact, their motivations will turn out to be surprisingly mundane — they're Intrigued by Humanity and want to see how we operate. How do we keep from getting cold without any fur? Where does our food come from if we don't have access to Functional Magic ? And how do we... reproduce? This usually non-malicious character type is regularly Played for Laughs , because it's hard to beat the humor of someone who doesn't understand human customs trying to make any sort of sense out of them. Defamiliarization, presenting audiences with familiar things seen in a strange way, can also be a form of social commentary. An outsider may wonder why humans will go to such lengths for Worthless Yellow Rocks , etc. The Fantastic Anthropologist very rarely has long-term story implications because often, part of their job is to keep the Muggles in the dark about what's going on. In terms of narrative, the Fantastic Anthropologist's culture can sometimes be a satire of our own. After all, as wacky as this guy may seem, when it gets right down to it we're not so different. Depending on the point of view of the story can often come off as Captain Oblivious . Frequently an Amusing Alien character. Compare Fantastic Science , Crazy Cultural Comparison , Intrigued by Humanity , The Xenophile . A subtrope of The Watcher . Humans Through Alien Eyes may be subject to this.
